subject: Astronomy & Space
title: The Big Crunch
description: A 60-second lesson on The Big Crunch.

hook.text: What if gravity wins? The Big Crunch is a scenario where the universe collapses back into a single point.

buildup.text: If there were enough matter, expansion would slow, stop, then reverse. Everything would crash together.

discovery.text: For decades, cosmologists debated whether this was our fate. Measuring the universe's density was the key test.

twist.text: The 1998 discovery of accelerating expansion ruled out the Big Crunch—at least under current conditions.

climax.text: Some speculative models allow dark energy to weaken over time, reopening the possibility in the far future.

punchline.text: The universe ending where it began. Poetic, but probably not happening.

quiz.question: Why is the Big Crunch considered unlikely?
options: The discovery of accelerating expansion means gravity won't reverse the expansion
The universe doesn't have enough mass to collapse
Dark matter prevents the universe from contracting
